results.label: Confident — GDPR hiring AI, EU
results.input: We are building an AI tool that screens job applicants and ranks them by suitability. We operate in Germany and France.

domains.reasoning: Explicit AI/ML screening system processing personal data (applicant information) for ranking purposes.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Operations explicitly stated in Germany and France, both EU member states subject to GDPR.

sector.reasoning: Tool explicitly designed for job applicant screening and ranking.

results.label: Confident — US health app
results.input: Our iOS app lets patients in the United States track chronic conditions, log medications, and share data with their doctors.

domains.reasoning: Patient health data processing is inherently privacy-sensitive; AI/ML is commonly used for health trend analysis and pattern recognition in chronic condition apps.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated United States user base and patient health data fall under HIPAA and FDA oversight.

data_types.reasoning: App explicitly processes patient health data (medications, condition logs) plus likely name/contact info for doctor sharing.

sector.reasoning: Patient chronic condition tracking and doctor data sharing is clearly healthcare sector.

results.label: Confident — UK fintech credit scoring
results.input: We provide algorithmic credit scoring to UK banks. Our model uses transaction history and behavioural signals to approve or decline loan applications.

domains.reasoning: Credit scoring involves personal data processing (privacy), algorithmic decision-making (ai), and financial systems typically have network/API exposure (cyber).

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicitly stated as serving UK banks.

data_types.reasoning: Transaction history is financial data; behavioural signals are explicitly mentioned as model inputs.

sector.reasoning: Credit scoring and loan decision-making are core financial services.

results.label: Confident — children's education platform, US
results.input: We run an online learning platform for K-12 students in the United States. We collect names, grades, usage data, and video recordings of tutoring sessions.

domains.reasoning: Platform processes personal data (names, grades, usage data, video recordings) of minors; likely uses algorithmic systems for learning personalization or recommendation.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicit mention of K-12 students in the United States; subject to FERPA (federal) and state education/privacy laws.

data_types.reasoning: Collects data on minors (children); grades and usage data are behavioural; names are general personal identifiers.

sector.reasoning: Online learning platform serving K-12 students is explicitly education sector.

results.label: Confident — EU smart city surveillance
results.input: Our computer vision platform processes CCTV footage across public spaces in Amsterdam and Brussels to monitor crowd density and detect incidents in real time.

domains.reasoning: Computer vision processes personal data (facial features in CCTV), uses AI/ML algorithms, and operates networked infrastructure with real-time data transmission.

jurisdictions.reasoning: Explicit deployment in Amsterdam and Brussels triggers GDPR and local Dutch/Belgian data protection laws; no other jurisdictions mentioned.

data_types.reasoning: CCTV footage captures facial/physical features (biometric) and precise locations of individuals in public spaces; crowd density monitoring inherently processes location data.

sector.reasoning: Public space monitoring and incident detection suggest government/municipal or transport authority use case; could also support security/safety operations in transport hubs.
